title: Contraception counseling

examination.text: Defines method-specific examination and test classification, preserves medical eligibility as a separate assessment, and supports pregnancy-status review before method initiation.
examination.rationale: Directly supports mapped pregnancy-status and generic eligibility-history review without supplying patient-specific eligibility conclusions.
examination.evidence_extract: Defines method-specific examination and test classification, preserves medical eligibility as a separate assessment, and supports pregnancy-status review before method initiation.

source.title: Selected practice recommendations for contraceptive use, fourth edition

exact_section.heading: 3.1 Examinations and tests; 3.2 Contraceptive eligibility; 5.1 Reasonable certainty that a woman is not pregnant

assessment.text: Defines the guideline as practical contraceptive-use guidance for providers once medical eligibility has been established.
assessment.rationale: Directly supports mapped contraception consultation, method use, initiation, continuation, and switching terminology after eligibility is established.
assessment.evidence_extract: Defines the guideline as practical contraceptive-use guidance for providers once medical eligibility has been established.

source.title: Selected practice recommendations for contraceptive use, fourth edition

assessment.text: Requires understandable information, voluntary informed choice, client preference, consent, and freedom from coercion.
assessment.rationale: Directly supports mapped client preference, informed choice, consent, and chosen-method documentation.
assessment.evidence_extract: Requires understandable information, voluntary informed choice, client preference, consent, and freedom from coercion.

source.title: Selected practice recommendations for contraceptive use, fourth edition

follow_up.text: Specifies method information on effectiveness, correct use, common effects, risks and benefits, return-to-clinic signs, fertility, STI protection, and opportunities for questions.
follow_up.rationale: Directly supports mapped counselling, options, benefits and risks, questions, STI protection, and clinician-discussed follow-up information.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Specifies method information on effectiveness, correct use, common effects, risks and benefits, return-to-clinic signs, fertility, STI protection, and opportunities for questions.

source_limitations: Full medical eligibility, smoking, migraine, thromboembolic, medication, and method-specific contraindication assessment requires the current WHO MEC and local clinician review.
UAE consent, prescribing, and service-delivery requirements are not established by this global source.
Fixed follow-up intervals and medication selection remain unsupported.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
